productStatement: GrantLayer turns agentic grant workflows into verifiable institutional records.

configurationCategories.name: Runtime mode
configurationCategories.purpose: Declares the active runtime mode so the product core and extensions can validate behavior and reject unsafe defaults.
configurationCategories.notes: Must be validated on startup. Invalid or missing mode must fail closed in production.

configurationCategories.name: Database backend
configurationCategories.purpose: Selects the persistence backend (SQLite, PostgreSQL, or future backends) and validates connectivity.
configurationCategories.notes: SQLite is acceptable for local-dev and test. PostgreSQL must be explicit and CI-gated for production.

configurationCategories.name: Persistence path / connection
configurationCategories.purpose: Specifies the file path or connection string for the active database backend.
configurationCategories.notes: Connection strings may contain credentials and must not be logged or exposed in error messages.

configurationCategories.name: Admin/operator access
configurationCategories.purpose: Defines who can mutate Product Core state and how roles are assigned and enforced.
configurationCategories.notes: Demo admin tokens are acceptable only in local-dev and demo. Production requires explicit role model.

configurationCategories.name: Authentication provider
configurationCategories.purpose: Configures the identity and authentication mechanism (local token, OAuth, JWT, SSO, mTLS).
configurationCategories.notes: Auth provider configuration must be explicit in production. Demo tokens must not persist.

configurationCategories.name: Secret management
configurationCategories.purpose: Defines how keys, tokens, and credentials are sourced, rotated, and stored.
configurationCategories.notes: Production requires managed secrets with rotation and access control. No synthetic keypairs allowed.

configurationCategories.name: Signing/key management
configurationCategories.purpose: Configures how Ed25519 or future signing keys are managed, rotated, and accessed.
configurationCategories.notes: Software signing is acceptable for pilots. HSM is a future upgrade path.

configurationCategories.name: Evidence storage
configurationCategories.purpose: Specifies where evidence bundles are persisted and how durability is guaranteed.
configurationCategories.notes: Local file paths are acceptable for local-dev and test. Production requires durable, explicit storage.

configurationCategories.name: Evidence verification strategy
configurationCategories.purpose: Selects the default and any additional verification strategies for evidence integrity.
configurationCategories.notes: SHA-256 recomparison is the current default. Additional strategies may be layered in future.

configurationCategories.name: Audit/provenance retention
configurationCategories.purpose: Defines how long audit events and provenance records are retained.
configurationCategories.notes: Retention policy must be explicit in production to satisfy institutional and regulatory expectations.

configurationCategories.name: Compliance/readiness profile
configurationCategories.purpose: Specifies which compliance dimensions are enforced and how readiness is evaluated.
configurationCategories.notes: Readiness evaluation must be documented in production so partners understand scope and limits.

configurationCategories.name: Policy/rule pack profile
configurationCategories.purpose: Specifies which policy rule packs are active and where they are loaded from.
configurationCategories.notes: Rule packs are configuration, not code changes, but must be explicit in production.

configurationCategories.name: API server binding / network exposure
configurationCategories.purpose: Defines host, port, and whether the server is exposed beyond localhost.
configurationCategories.notes: Local-only binding is acceptable for local-dev, test, and demo. Production requires explicit and restricted exposure.

configurationCategories.name: CORS / external access
configurationCategories.purpose: Specifies which origins and external clients are permitted to access the API.
configurationCategories.notes: Permissive CORS is acceptable in local-dev and demo. Production requires explicit origin allowlisting.

configurationCategories.name: Logging / observability
configurationCategories.purpose: Configures structured logging, metrics, alerting, and tracing.
configurationCategories.notes: Logs must not contain secrets or credentials. Structured logging is required in production.

configurationCategories.name: Backup / restore
configurationCategories.purpose: Defines automated backup schedule, retention, point-in-time recovery, and disaster runbooks.
configurationCategories.notes: Backup and restore must be explicit, tested, and documented in production.

configurationCategories.name: Feature flags / extension toggles
configurationCategories.purpose: Controls which extensions and experimental features are enabled.
configurationCategories.notes: Experimental features should be disabled by default in production and enabled only with explicit approval.

configurationCategories.name: External integrations
configurationCategories.purpose: Configures which external services are connected and how they are authenticated.
configurationCategories.notes: External integrations must be explicit in production. Credentials must be managed through the secret management boundary.

configurationCategories.name: Demo/test fixture mode
configurationCategories.purpose: Controls whether synthetic fixtures are loaded and how they are isolated from real data.
configurationCategories.notes: Fixture mode must be disabled in production. Fixtures must never mix with institutional data.

configurationCategories.name: Safe failure behavior
configurationCategories.purpose: Defines how the system behaves when configuration is missing, invalid, or untrusted.
configurationCategories.notes: Production must fail closed. Local and test modes may fail open for developer convenience, but this must be explicit.

failClosedRules: Production mode must refuse to start if runtime mode is missing or invalid.
Production mode must refuse to start if database backend is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if auth provider is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if secret management is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if signing/key management is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if evidence storage is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if observability/logging is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if backup/restore is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if network binding is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if CORS/external access is not explicitly configured.
Production mode must refuse to start if demo/test fixture mode is enabled.
Missing or invalid required configuration must produce a clear, structured error without leaking secrets.
sensitiveValueRules: Secrets must not be written into evidence bundles.
Secrets must not be written into audit event records.
Secrets must not be written into provenance summaries.
Secrets must not be written into logs or error messages.
Secrets must not be included in examples or exports.
Connection strings containing credentials must be masked in logs and error messages.
Signing keys must never be exposed through the API or stored in the database unencrypted.
Demo tokens and synthetic keypairs must not be present in production configuration.
extensionConfigurationBoundaries: Extensions must declare their configuration requirements explicitly.
Extensions must not read arbitrary environment variables directly.
Extensions must receive configuration through the same validated boundary as Product Core.
Extensions must fail closed when their required configuration is missing.
Extensions must not leak secrets into Product Core records or logs.
Adapter behavior must be configurable, not hard-coded.
implementationNonGoals: Secret manager integration (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, etc.)
Auth provider integration (OAuth, JWT, SSO, mTLS)
